title: Working in Belgium
description: Work permits, taxes, banking, salary expectations and social security in Belgium.

items.title: Work Permit / Single Permit
items.description: Since January 2019, work and residence authorisation for non-EU nationals staying over 90 days is combined into a single unified 'Single Permit' (Type B for standard employees), applied for by the employer through the relevant region's one-stop counter.
steps: Secure a job offer from a Belgian employer
Employer files the Single Permit application with the competent region (Flanders, Wallonia or Brussels-Capital — each has its own salary thresholds)
Once approved, apply for the corresponding long-stay (Type D) visa in India
Register with your local municipality within 8 working days of arrival
tips: The Single Permit is tied to the employer that filed it — changing jobs requires a new application
Typical processing time is around 4-8 weeks; government fees are generally €150-250 depending on region and permit type
The EU Blue Card is an alternative for highly-skilled roles meeting salary thresholds

items.title: Taxes
items.description: Belgium has a progressive personal income tax system administered by the FPS Finance (Federal Public Service Finance / SPF Finances); residents file an annual tax return.
tips: Belgium has a Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement (DTAA) with India
Tax rates and brackets change periodically — verify current figures directly with FPS Finance (finance.belgium.be) rather than relying on older guides

items.title: Banking
items.description: Opening a Belgian bank account is usually needed for salary payment and daily life.
steps: Get your national registry number via municipality registration
Choose a bank (e.g. KBC, BNP Paribas Fortis, ING, Belfius)
Bring your passport, residence proof and employment contract to open an account

items.title: Salary
items.description: Salary levels vary significantly by sector, role and region — Brussels and Antwerp generally offer higher wages than smaller cities, and regional salary thresholds also affect Single Permit eligibility.
tips: Check the current regional salary threshold for your Single Permit category before negotiating an offer, as it can affect visa eligibility

items.title: Social Security
items.description: Employees and employers contribute to Belgium's social security system, covering healthcare, pensions and unemployment benefits.
tips: India and Belgium have a Social Security Agreement covering certain contribution exemptions for posted workers
